layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1. Radiation Safety Legislation and Code of Practice

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1.1 Radiation Safety Legislation, Code of Practice and Rules

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Besides the general occupational health and safety (OHS） practice, working with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sealed/unsealed radiation sources in NSW is regulated under the NSW Radiation Control

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Regulation (2003). Australian Standard 2234.4 - 1994 and Code of Practice for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Protection Against Ionising Radiation Emitted from X-ray Analysis Equipment (1984).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: We have a statutory obligation to comply with the requirements and practices described

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: in these documents. The documents can be access through UNsW Library's online

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: services.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The University of New South Wales has developed its own radiation policy - “Radiation

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Safety Policy and Program - Part A Ionising Radiation". It is accessible from the UNSW

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: web site (http://www.riskman.unsw.edu.au/ohs/radiationsafety.shtml). The policy clearly

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: outlines the all practical requirements and responsibilities of those, who are working with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: radiation sources or supervising the use of radiation sources in UNSW. All XRD users

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: must read this policy before starting their XRD work.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: All users (licensed and licensing excepted) must undertake a training program for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: radiation safety and instrument operation before they can use X-ray diffraction units in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the XRD laboratory. Only qualified and licensed staff can provide this type of training.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The training program must have appropriate record of trainer/trainee, date and associated.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Trainee should obtain a copy of radiation guidelines (Management Document OO1/2007)

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and standard operating procedure for specified XRD unit.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The X-ray diffraction laboratory in the Analytical Centre UNSW has fully complied with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: all of the above legislation, codes and policies. All users/operators have responsibility to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: follow these continuously.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1.2 Radiation Licensing and Training

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: All intending users of ionising radiation sources must first obtain a radiation licence from

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the Environment Protection Authority (EPA) (http://www.epa.nsw.gov.au/home.htm).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: This applies to most users. The licence will indicate the type of radiation sources to be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: permitted, where appropriate and conditions.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: An exception to this rule is all users working with sealed radiation sources at a fixed

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: location. In this case,a Licensing Exception Approval has to be provided in writing,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: identifying the student, the supervisor and the details of the work to be taken and any

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: conditions associated with it. Using X-ray diffraction units, at X-ray diffraction

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: laboratory, Analytical Centre UNSW, is licensing excepted.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: X-ray Diffraction (XRD) Laboratory， Analytical Centre, UNSW

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1.4 Risk Assessment

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Management Document 0o1/2007

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: March 2007

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1.5 Radiation Safety Information Board in XRD Laboratory

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In the school of Materials Science and Engineering, radiation safety supervisor is Dr. Yu

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Wang (x54527). UNSW radiation safety coordinator is Bob Armstrong (x52912). Any

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: issue related to radiation safety must be passed to those people directly.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Before a new user can start his/her own XRD test, a risk assessment must be carried out.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: After that the user must fill the Risk assessment form and submit to the radiation safety

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: supervisor and the safety officer at Analytical Centre.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ·Approving standard operating procedures;

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The Radiation Safety Information Board in the XRD Laboratory provides all information

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: about radiation safety，which includes licenced staff names， student names under

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: radiation licensing exception， radiation footprint， emergency evacuation procedure,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: incident reporting procedure and radiation legislation documents.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1.3 Radiation Supervision

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In the XRD laboratory, only qualified staff can provide radiation supervision. Their

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: names have be displayed on the radiation safety information board in the lab.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The responsibilities of the Radiation Safety Supervisor include.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ·Training users for radiation safety and standard operation;

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Obtain and maintain knowledge of the principles and practices of protection

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: against radiation and of potential hazards;

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ·Approving student exception to access XRD units, and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Identifying local radiation risk areas.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: X-ray Diffraction (XRD) Laboratory， Analytical Centre, UNSW

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Management Document 0o1/2007

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: March 2007

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The IS measurement of X-ray intensity is the Sievert (Sv)， corresponding to the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: absorption of one joule in one kilogram of biological matter, taking into account the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: quality factor and other modifying factors.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Standard limits of effective dose are 2O mSv/year for occupational and 1 mSv/year for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: general public. Although the standard prescribe limits on an annual basis, it is useful to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ensure that doses received do not exceed lmSv/week. It should note that natural

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2.1 Characteristics of X-ray Radiation

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: X-ray radiation is harmful to the human body. A localised dose is sufficient to cause a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: severe radiation burn (human tissues are killed). Doses are also accumulated in the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: human body by long term exposed to radiation that produce irradiated cells. The hazards

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: include an increased risk of leukaemia, cancer and genetic or hereditary effects.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: X-ray's wavelength, used with XRD, is around 1 - 4 A. They are invisible to naked eye

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and strongly penetrative. However， they are generated by electric power. Once the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: electric power being cut off, X-rays will completely vanish and no radioactive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: contamination retains in the area, no radiation hazard exists.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The radiation monitor measures the radiation in terms of rate - μSv/hr (micro-Sievert per

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: hour). Normal background radiation level in our XRD lab is on the order of O.2 -0.5

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: μSv/hr. At this rate, one would expect to receive maximum 0.5x 24 hr = 12 μSv of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: exposure per day or 0.5x24x365 = 4.38 mSv/year.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Injury may occur to the operator and/or other personnel close to X-ray equipment due to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: exposure to a primary beam or leakage or scattered radiation.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Three major factors are considered in preventing radiation hazard - time, distance and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: shielding.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2.2 Radiation monitoring and Permitted dose

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2. Working with X-ray Diffraction Instruments

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: X-rays are a very energetic form of electromagnetic radiation that will ionise matter with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: which they interact, by ejecting electrons from their atoms. They are classified as sealed

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: radiation sources， when devices or materials, which generate X-rays，have been

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: permanently bonded or fixed in a capsule or tube to prevent release and dispersal of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: radiation under the most severe conditions,and unsealed radiation sources such as

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: radioactive substances. The X-rays used in X-ray diffraction (XRD) unit is the sealed

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: radiation sources which is enclosed in X-ray tube housing and produced only when they

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: are energized.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: X-ray Diffraction (XRD) Laboratory, Analytical Centre, UNSW

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Management Document 0o1/2007

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: March 2007

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: X-ray tube housing Each X-ray tube is enclosed in a tube housing that can not be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: fractured or deformed by normal use, accidental impact or misuse.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: X-ray energised warning indicator (light) indicates X-ray tube is in operation

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and X-ray are generated, but they could be enclosed in the tube housing only.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2.4 Understanding Lab safety Features and Requirements

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: A radiation monitor is provided in the XRD laboratory. All users need to know how to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: use the monitor. The results from lab inspections show on the Radiation Safety

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Information Board.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: background radiation to which everybody is exposed from the environment is in an order

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of 2 mSv/year.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: All X-ray difraction instruments have equipped with many radiation protection features.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Understanding of these features is the most important step to ensure radiation safety.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: It can only be used in case of emergency.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2.3 Radiation Protection Features in XRD Instrument

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The tube housing shutter (X-ray beam stop) is placed close to the housing

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: aperture so that to attenuate the radiation doses. The shutter is opened only when

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: measurement starts.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Shutter warning indicator If it is on, it indicates the shuter is opened. X-rays

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: are in the working chamber. In this case no one is allowed to open the door of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: chamber.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Interlocks Many microswitches are fit on each XRD system to ensure that the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sample chamber has been properly enclosed before shutter opens. Any attempt to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: open the chamber, when the shutter is opened, will lead to system de-energised

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: immediately.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Sample chamber It houses the test samples and prevents accessing the primary

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: X-ray beams. The chamber is constructed of appropriate materials to attenuate X-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ray radiation during measurement.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Push-off button (Red Push Button) each XRD unit has a push-off button to shut

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: down electric power. They are all labeled with red “Power Off’ whatever its

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: location and type. Users should know its location and push it in emergency case.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Service/disruption Notice (Yellow Note) A yellow note is stored in the operating

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: procedure folder of each unit. When user finds a problem of operation or safety,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: please shut down the unit, put the note in front of the unit and then inform lab

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: safety supervisor.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: X-ray Diffraction (XRD) Laboratory, Analytical Centre, UNSW

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Management Document 0o1/2007

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: March 2007

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The lab can not be used as sample preparation room and not store any type of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: samples.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The operator or any other person involved in the use of XRD equipment should undergo

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: a medical examination following any known or suspected occasion when the person has

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: been exposed to a primary beam from XRD equipment.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 2.5 Medical Requirements

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: When user find a yellow note has been placed to a XRD unit, don't use that unit

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: until a school technical staff takes it off.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Users are not allowed to do any repair， adjustment or modification of XRD

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: hardware. Anyone, who tries to do so, will be dismissed from use XRDs in the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: laboratory.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Safe Samples To maintain a safe environment of the lab, the safety supervisor

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: may ask users to provide certain information of their samples. Users should insure

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that their samples are safety samples, which means that to performing a XRD test

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: will not lead to pollute or contaminate the lab.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: User must report to safety supervisor if their samples may contain radioactive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: substances, nano-powders, something which may produce toxic gases under the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: low heat, or lead to contaminate instrument. Without taking necessary

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: precautions, user can not perform any test to those samples.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: X-ray Diffraction (XRD) Laboratory， Analytical Centre, UNSW

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Management Document 0o1/2007

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: March 2007

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In the case of a building emergency, switch off the equipment completely before

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: leaving the laboratory.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Radiation Safety Rules for Use of XRD Instruments

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Shutter indicator lights on is to say there are X-ray in the chamber.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1.1 Check the placard on the door to know main potential hazards, the personal

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: protective equipment to be worn and the names and phone numbers of the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: persons to be contacted for emergencies.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1.2 No person is permitted to operate XRD equipment unless they have taken

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: training of radiation safety and operating procedures, and done risk assessment.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: All users must follow the standard operating procedure to use XRD unit.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1.5 Turn off the equipment immediately when potentially hazardous situations

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: occur arising from X-ray beam, due to any reason. Report to the radiation safety

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: supervisor any actual or suspected case of radiation exposure.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1.6 In the case of radiation exposure incident, you must follow “Hazard and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Incident Reporting and Investigation Procedure” on the radiation safety

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: information board or http://www.riskman.unsw.edu.au/ohs/hazard.shtml.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1.3 Check “Service/Disruption Notice” (Yellow note). If one has been placed to a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: XRD unit, don't use that instrument until technical staff takes it out.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1.4 Check shutter indicator before opening the door of sample chamber. If the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: indicator lights on, don't open the door of chamber at any circumstance.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1.7 Recording your usage time, with actual operator's name (not research group), in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the specific XRD logbook after use.
